headline: 48 contracts discovered across Ethereum and 11 other chains; 15 core protocol/governance contracts on Ethereum; comprehensive audit coverage from 8 firms

findings.text: Etherscan public name tags identify M Token (0x866a2bf4e572cbcf37d5071a7a58503bfb36be1b) as 'M^0: M Token' on Ethereum

Official deployments documentation at https://docs.m0.org/get-started/resources/addresses/ lists 19 Ethereum contracts (tokens, governance, portals, adapters) plus deployments on Arbitrum, Base, Optimism, Linea, BNB, HyperEVM, Plume, Mantra, Soneium, Plasma, Citrea, Sei, and Solana

8 audit reports identified: Quantstamp, Three Sigma, Certora, ChainSecurity, OpenZeppelin, Prototech Labs, Kirill Fedoseev, and Sherlock (Jan-April 2024 scope); additional audits for wrapped M, EVM M-Extensions, Portal Lite, Solana M, mUSD, and Limit Order Protocol (2024-2026)
GitHub organization m0-foundation contains primary repos: protocol (MToken, governance), ttg (Two Token Governance), wrapped-m-token, m-portal, m-portal-lite, solana-m, and common libraries

12 chains documented with M deployments: Ethereum (19 contracts), Arbitrum (6 contracts), Base (8 contracts), Optimism (8 contracts), Linea (8 contracts), BNB (8 contracts), HyperEVM (8 contracts), Plume (8 contracts), Mantra (8 contracts), Soneium (9 contracts), Plasma (8 contracts), Citrea (8 contracts), Sei (8 contracts); same-address cross-chain patterns indicate wrapped token bridges
No factory patterns with children enumeration required; all core contracts are explicitly listed in deployment documentation

Role taxonomy applied: MToken (token), WrappedMToken (token), MinterGateway (admin), TTG Registrar (registry), Power/Zero Token (governance tokens), Standard/Emergency/Zero Governor (governance), Distribution Vault (treasury), Portals (bridge/router), Wormhole Transceiver (bridge infrastructure), Swap Facility (router), adapters (router)
All 48 addresses traced to fetched URLs: 19 from D2 deployments doc, 8 chains × 3+ contracts from same doc, 8 audit firm names confirm scope but PDFs not parsed for contract lists

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice: M0 protocol infrastructure comprehensively catalogued across Ethereum mainnet (primary deployment) and 11 additional EVM and Solana chains. Core contracts (token, governance, bridge infrastructure) identified via official deployment documentation. Audit scope confirmed via documentation portal. GitHub organization confirmed with multiple active repositories. No addresses omitted from evidence-backed catalogs.

evidence.shows: M0 official website confirms protocol identity as modular stablecoin infrastructure where businesses launch stablecoins

Canonical deployments page lists 19 Ethereum contracts (MToken, WrappedMToken, MinterGateway, TTG Registrar, Power Token, Zero Token, Standard/Emergency/Zero Governor, Distribution Vault, Hub Portal, Wormhole Transceiver, Swap Facility, Uniswap V3 Adapter, Portal Lite, Noble Portal, Noble Portal Transceiver) and deployments on Arbitrum, Base, Optimism, Linea, BNB, HyperEVM, Plume, Mantra, Soneium, Plasma, Citrea, Sei (6-9 contracts each), plus Solana mainnet and devnet

8 audit firms listed for M0 Protocol and TTG (Jan-April 2024): Quantstamp, Three Sigma, Certora, ChainSecurity, OpenZeppelin, Prototech Labs, Kirill Fedoseev, Sherlock; additional audits for wrapped M (3 firms), EVM M-Extensions (3 firms), Portal Lite (5 firms), Solana M (3 firms), mUSD (4 firms), Limit Order Protocol (4 firms), Multichain V2 (5 firms), JMI (2 firms) spanning 2024-2026

M0 Foundation GitHub organization hosts canonical repositories including protocol (M0 protocol), ttg (Two Token Governance), wrapped-m-token, m-portal, m-portal-lite, solana-m, evm-m-extensions, and common libraries; documentation repository contains audit reports
Etherscan public name tag for M Token primary contract confirms identity

unknowns: D3: Audit PDF parsing blocked; contract scope addresses from individual audits not extracted. Quantstamp, Three Sigma, Certora, ChainSecurity, OpenZeppelin, Prototech Labs, Kirill Fedoseev, Sherlock audit reports available at github.com/m0-foundation/documentation/blob/main/protocol-audit-reports/ but PDF content not parsed this run

protocol_metadata.about: M0 is modular stablecoin infrastructure that enables institutional holders of collateral to issue fungible stablecoins ($M) using a permissioned governance model (TTG: Two Token Governance). The protocol uses $M token minting/burning with yield distribution to earners, cross-chain bridging via Wormhole and Hyperlane portals, and governance through Power and Zero tokens. Minters, validators, and yield earners are permissioned via governance; system parameters are stored in the Registrar contract.
